Mutual Non-Disclosure Deed



between



Foundr



and

ABC



                           





________________________________________________________________________________



































Friday, October 18, 2019





MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE DEED

	



PARTIES: 	



Foundr



And 



ABC







RECITALS



Each Party proposes to make confidential information available to the other Party for a specified purpose.

Each Party agrees to maintain the secrecy of the other party’s confidential information on the terms and conditions of this deed.



OPERATIVE PROVISIONS



DEFINITIONS



The following definitions apply in this deed, unless the contrary intention appears:



“Authorised Person” means any officer, employee, agent or contractor of the Recipient or any of its related bodies corporate or related persons.



“Foundr” means Foundr



“Confidential Information” means any information that is, during the term of this deed, of a confidential nature and not a matter of common knowledge, including but not limited to business, marketing, technical, financial and scientific information, designs, drawings, and other communicable information that is valuable to either Party or to any third party in whatever form owned by a Party or any of its related bodies corporate or related persons and provided by that Party to another Party, directly or indirectly, including, but not limited to, its business schedules, manpower planning, product specifications and descriptions, budgets, finances or plans, designs and licenses of computer hardware and software, research, development, distribution and marketing of programs for software, websites, website development, software tools and methodologies, business relationships partnerships or business joint ventures, on or after the date of this deed.



“Derived Information” means information in any form or media which is derived or produced by the Recipient using Confidential Information in whole or in part.



“Discloser” means a Party who makes Confidential Information available to another Party.



“Intellectual Property Rights” means any and all existing and future intellectual or industrial property rights (whether registered or unregistered) including, without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, all existing and future patents, copyrights, design rights, database rights, trade marks, internet rights/domain names, know-how, confidential information and any and all applications for any of the foregoing and any and all rights to apply for any of the foregoing. 



“Party” means a signatory to this document.



”Permitted Purpose” means the purpose of evaluating a possible commercial relationship between the Discloser and the Recipient, and, if the Parties are agreed to proceed with that commercial relationship, the purpose of undertaking that commercial relationship.



“Recipient” means, in relation to a Discloser, a Party that receives Confidential Information from that Discloser.



“Records” means reproduction of Confidential Information in any form or media.



“related body corporate” has the same meaning as in the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth) or similar subsequent legislation.



“related persons” means, where a Party is not a corporation within the meaning of the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth) or similar subsequent legislation, any unincorporated entity or person immediately related to that Party by blood or marriage including but not limited to spouse, children, parent, step-child, step-parent and sibling.  



NON-DISCLOSURE OBLIGATIONS



2.1	Recipient’s obligations



Each recipient agrees that:



any and all written or oral Confidential Information disclosed by the Discloser will be treated by the Recipient as secret and confidential;

it can only use the Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose;

subject to clause 2.1(e), it must not disclose any confidential Information to any third Party nor use such Confidential Information for any purpose not contemplated by this deed without the prior written consent of the Discloser;

it must take all reasonable care necessary to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information including taking all reasonable steps necessary to secure the Confidential Information against theft, loss or unauthorised disclosure;

it can only disclose the Confidential Information to its Authorised Persons on a need to know basis, and only if the Recipient notifies the Authorised Persons of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the Authorised Persons agree to be bound by the same obligations of confidentiality as the Recipient in accordance with clause 2.2(c); and

it will take reasonable steps to ensure that no other person has access to the Confidential Information other than in accordance with this deed.



2.2	Discussions Between the Parties



Each Party agrees:



not to disclose to any person other than the Authorised Persons, the fact that any discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Parties; 

not to disclose to any person other than the Authorised Persons any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to the discussions and negotiations taking place between the Parties; and



2.3	Obligations of the Parties



The obligations of the Parties under this deed expire on the second anniversary of date of this deed.



EXCEPTIONS TO OBLIGATIONS OF CONFIDENCE



The obligations imposed on a Recipient under this deed do not apply to Confidential Information which:



becomes available through no wrongful act or breach of this deed by the Recipient;

is published prior to the date of this deed;

was developed by the Recipient independent of the disclosure by the Discloser;

the Recipient can prove by contemporaneous written documentation was already known to it at the time of disclosure by the Discloser (other than if such knowledge arose from a disclosure of information in breach of an obligation of confidentiality);

is received from a third Party without restriction and without breach of this or any other non disclosure deed;

the Discloser has given its written consent to disclose or use; or

is required to be disclosed:

by law or by any order of any court or tribunal or competent jurisdiction; or



by a requirement of any government agency, stock exchange or other regulatory body either having the legal right to so require or with which a responsible person in a similar position would comply,



but the Recipient must, as promptly as possible and where legally permissible, inform the Discloser when it becomes subject to such a disclosure requirement and consult with the Discloser as to the form and content of any disclosure so required.



GENERAL



4.1	Return or Destruction of Records



All Confidential Information is provided by the Discloser to the Recipient for use solely in connection with the terms of this deed, and shall be destroyed by the Recipient if requested to do so by the Discloser.



4.2	Remedies



Each Party acknowledges that damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this deed and the Discloser is entitled to seek specific performance or injunctive relief as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach by the Recipient in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.



4.3 	No representations or warranties



The Recipient acknowledges that the Discloser (including its Authorised Persons) has made no representation or warranty as to the accurateness or completeness of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees to make its own assessment of the Confidential Information and satisfy itself as to the accuracy and completeness of the Confidential Information.



4.4	No Rights to Confidential Information



Nothing contained in this deed may be construed as granting or confirming on the Recipient any proprietary rights, licenses or other rights in any Confidential Information.



4.5	Governing Law



This deed is governed by the law in force in Victoria, Australia

Each Party submits to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts exercising jurisdiction in Victoria and any court that may hear appeals from any of those courts, for any proceedings in connection with this deed, and waives any right it might have to claim that those courts are an inconvenient forum.



4.6	Further Assurances



Each party agrees, at its own expense, on the request of another Party, to do everything reasonably necessary to give effect to this deed and the transactions contemplated by it, but not limited to, the execution of agreements, and to use all reasonable endeavors to cause relevant third Parties to do likewise.



4.7	Entire Agreement



This deed contains the entire agreement between the Parties about its specific subject matter. Any previous understanding, agreement, representation or warranty relating to that subject matter is replaced by this deed and has no further effect.





Counterparts

This deed may be executed in counterparts.





INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY



4.1	Intellectual Property Rights



During your time with Foundr, you may either alone or in conjunction with others generate or assist in the generation of documents, materials, designs, drawings, processes, formulae, computer coding, methodologies, confidential information and other works which relate to Foundr’s business or which are capable of being used or adapted for use therein or in connection therewith (“Works”) and you agree that in respect of any such Works and all Intellectual Property Rights in relation thereto, you are obliged to further the interests of Foundr. Both the Works and the related Intellectual Property Rights will belong to and be the absolute property of Foundr of any other person Foundr may nominate. 



You must immediately disclose to your Business Head all Works and all related Intellectual Property Rights, you are required to assist Foundr and fully cooperate with it in every way to enforce Foundr’s right to execute whatever documentation is required to provide Foundr with full ownership thereof. 









Executed as a deed:





Representing Foundr

Signed by:
